name: Sun Bin

skills.name: Hourglass of Time
skills.description: Gains a Movement Speed bonus after using skills.

skills.name: Time Bomb
skills.description: Fires a bomb in the target direction, which deals damage and attaches itself to the hit enemy. After a brief delay, inflicts AoE damage and slow.

skills.name: Time Flux
skills.description: Timebender and nearby teammates within range gain increased Movement Speed. When the effect ends, restores Health equal to 25% of the damage taken while the effect was active. Passive: Gains Cooldown Reduction.

skills.name: Time Lapse
skills.description: Throws a bomb in the target direction, inflicting AoE damage, slow, and silence upon coming in contact with an enemy or reaching its max range. After a brief delay, the bomb deals AoE damage again.

tips: Sun Bin is a Easy Support (Roaming) on Honor of Kings Global. Camp HOK ranks them Tier B with 50.2% win rate and 0.4% pick rate as of 2026-06-05.
Best build path: Boots of Tranquility → Glacial Buckler → Succubus Cloak → Venomous Staff → Sage's Sanctuary.
Run Nightmare for stats that match Sun Bin's Support kit. Take Heal — swap to Purify against heavy CC or Smite when jungling.
Standard trade: Time Bomb → Time Flux → Time Lapse → reposition. Weave basic attacks between skill casts for maximum damage and passive value.
Laning: Help marksman crash wave, then rotate mid for picks. Return before Tyrant if your carry has no escape.
Teamfight: Peel carry first, then look for pick on out-of-position enemies. Ward Tyrant 30s before spawn.
Sun Bin excels into Kaizer, Musashi. Avoid blind picking into Wukong, Musashi, Ying without team peel.
Sun Bin sits at Tier B globally (50.2% WR). In high rank, pick only into favorable matchups or when you have a dedicated pocket pick.

faqs.question: Is Sun Bin good in the current meta?
faqs.answer: Sun Bin is Tier B Support with 50.2% win rate, 0.4% pick rate, and 0.0% ban rate on Honor of Kings Global (Camp HOK, 2026-06-05). Niche — draft carefully.

faqs.question: What is the best Sun Bin build?
faqs.answer: Core item path: Boots of Tranquility, Glacial Buckler, Succubus Cloak, Venomous Staff, Sage's Sanctuary, nightmare. Run Nightmare arcana with Heal. Switch to alternate build presets on this page for jungle, clash, or late-game setups.

faqs.question: How to counter Sun Bin?
faqs.answer: Pick Wukong, Musashi, Ying into Sun Bin. Deny Roaming resources early and force fights before Sun Bin completes core items. Sun Bin struggles when the enemy can out-range or out-sustain them.

faqs.question: Who does Sun Bin counter?
faqs.answer: Sun Bin is strong into Kaizer, Musashi according to HoKStats matchup data and role matchups on the global server. Draft Sun Bin when the enemy relies on these heroes in the Roaming lane.

faqs.question: Is Sun Bin good in high rank / Diamond+?
faqs.answer: Sun Bin sits at Tier B globally (50.2% WR). In high rank, pick only into favorable matchups or when you have a dedicated pocket pick.

faqs.question: What lane should Sun Bin play?
faqs.answer: Sun Bin is tagged Roaming in Camp export (Support, Mage). Help marksman crash wave, then rotate mid for picks. Return before Tyrant if your carry has no escape.

faqs.question: Best arcana and spells for Sun Bin?
faqs.answer: Recommended: Nightmare arcana. Battle spell: Heal. Adjust Purify/Execute based on enemy dive and jungle need.

faqs.question: Sun Bin or Dolia — which is better?
faqs.answer: Sun Bin vs Dolia (Support, Tier S): Dolia is 1.8% pick / 46.1% WR. Sun Bin has +4.1% WR vs Dolia in Camp data. Choose Sun Bin when you need Roaming identity; choose Dolia for Roaming comfort.

faqs.question: Should I ban Sun Bin?
faqs.answer: Low priority ban (0.0%). Focus bans on higher-tier Support picks unless targeting a one-trick.
